Featured on

“DeleteMe found that all six major people databases – 123people.com, MyLife.com, Spokeo, US Search, White Pages and PeopleFinder.com – have dossiers on me. All have my home address, which doesn’t thrill me…”

“The internet is literally an addiction and our online existence only expands the longer we perpetuate its use. But there is a way to end it all — sign up for DeleteMe and remove yourself from the hellscape that is the internet.”

“DeleteMe, one of the online reputation services, promised to remove me from the top people-finder databases — in my case, 23 of them. Over the next several weeks I watched in relief as my data disappeared. From some services I was erased within 24 hours.”

“You can limit how easy it is to use your information by removing it from certain sites online through services like DeleteMe, or through the time-consuming process of opting out yourself.”

“Organizations should offer concrete resources and services to employees: These should include: cybersecurity services that protect against impersonation, doxing, and identity theft such as Deleteme. Harvard Business Review, What to Do When Your Employee is Harassed Online“

Take Control of Your Online Privacy

Take Control of Your Online Privacy

Explore our comprehensive guides to opt out of data brokers, delete your accounts, and protect yourself from doxxing threats. Stay informed and safeguard your personal information in the digital age.

Opt-Out Guides

Take control of your online presence by opting out of data brokers. Our guides help you remove your personal information from sites that sell or share your data, keeping you more secure.
Read more

Doxxing Awareness and Protection

Learn how to safeguard yourself from doxxing attacks. Our comprehensive resources show you how to remove sensitive personal information from the internet before it can be exploited.
Read more

Delete Your Account

Reduce your digital footprint with our step-by-step guides to delete your accounts on popular platforms like Reddit, TikTok and Instagram. Protect your personal data and regain your privacy.

Read more

General
questions

  • What is a Data Broker?

    Data brokers are corporations that collect huge amounts of personally identifiable information (PII) and package it all together to create ‘profiles’ or ‘listings’ with your personal information. These profiles include things like Social Security numbers, birthdays, past and recent addresses, and more.
    Read More

  • How is my personal information online?

    Data brokers crawl the web searching for information, and use it to build a profile of you. They find this from government and other public records, self-reported information, social media, and other data brokers.
    Read More

  • What happens after I sign up for DeleteMe?

    Once you’ve completed your sign-up for DeleteMe, we’ll send you a welcome email so you can get started right away. You’ll log in and find your DeleteMe personal profile page. You tell us exactly what information you want deleted, and our privacy experts take it from there.
    Read More

  • Can you delete Google search results for me?

    We cannot delete Google search results themselves without first removing the source information that the search result is pulling the information from, the data broker websites. Google is not the source of the search results it’s showing you; it’s merely displaying your information from the most relevant sources, based on your Google search query letting your information be found more easily. Google does not have the file containing your personal information, nor can it delete the file.
    Read More

Uncover Your Digital Footprint

When you enter your name, city, and state into a search engine, you may be surprised at just how much of your private information is out there.

    questions
    • What is a Data Broker?

      Data brokers are corporations that collect huge amounts of personally identifiable information (PII) and package it all together to create ‘profiles’ or ‘listings’ with your personal information. These profiles include things like Social Security numbers, birthdays, past and recent addresses, and more.
      Read More
    • How is my personal information online?

      Data brokers crawl the web searching for information, and use it to build a profile of you. They find this from government and other public records, self-reported information, social media, and other data brokers.
      Read More
    • What happens after I sign up for DeleteMe?

      Once you’ve completed your sign-up for DeleteMe, we’ll send you a welcome email so you can get started right away. You’ll log in and find your DeleteMe personal profile page. You tell us exactly what information you want deleted, and our privacy experts take it from there.
      Read More
    • Can you delete Google search results for me?

      We cannot delete Google search results themselves without first removing the source information that the search result is pulling the information from, the data broker websites. Google is not the source of the search results it’s showing you; it’s merely displaying your information from the most relevant sources, based on your Google search query letting your information be found more easily. Google does not have the file containing your personal information, nor can it delete the file.
